pr.title: ft: Match ftPp_SpecialHi functions in ftPp_SpecialS.c
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Attempted to match several Ice Climbers/Popo SpecialHi routines in ftPp_SpecialS.c, including ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC, ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8, and ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0. The PR also cleaned up a conditional/M2C workaround in ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag, added a small matching stage helper in grinishie1.c, and flipped ftPp_SpecialS.c and grinishie1.c to Matching in configure.py. It was closed unmerged because the batched worktree commits had build issues that needed to be fixed at the source.
what_changed: Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter in ftPp_SpecialS.c, including GET_FIGHTER use, dat_attrs access, ground velocity scaling through an attribute at offset 0x84, transition into ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC, cmd_vars reset, mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0 initialization, fv.pp.x223C reset, and zeroing fv.pp.x2240 vector components.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC as a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call to motion state 0x15B with Ft_MF_None followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8 as a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call to motion state 0x160 with Ft_MF_None followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0 as a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call to motion state 0x15C.
Changed ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag to use named Fighter fields instead of SOLUTION/M2C_FIELD conditional code for x670_timer_lstick_tilt_x, x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y, and x221F_b4.
Changed the hitlag stick magnitude comparison from equality against cd_x4B0 squared to a greater-than-or-equal comparison using temporary x_sq and y_sq variables.

Added grInishie1_801FCAAC, a stage helper that checks stage_info.internal_stage_id == 0x18, rejects arg0 == -1, maps a line to a joint with mpJointFromLine, and returns true only for joints 0x14 or 0x15.
Changed configure.py to mark melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c as Matching, although the excerpt still shows a duplicate NonMatching entry for the same file immediately afterward.
Changed configure.py to mark melee/gr/grinishie1.c as Matching.
smart_moves: Replaced M2C_FIELD offset access in ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag with named struct fields, making the code more maintainable and searchable while preserving the same apparent data access intent.
Used small temporary variables x_sq and y_sq around the stick magnitude check, a common matching tactic when expression shape affects generated code.
Factored SpecialHi state transitions into short helper routines that directly mirror Fighter_ChangeMotionState plus ftAnim_8006EBA4 patterns.
In grInishie1_801FCAAC, expressed the joint range check as an unsigned range idiom, `(u32)(joint - 0x14) > 1`, which is often useful for matching compiler output for two adjacent accepted values.
decomp_lessons: Do not flip an object to Matching in configure.py until the full object builds and matches; this PR was closed because the batch had build issues.
When replacing M2C_FIELD accesses with named Fighter fields, verify the struct field names and bitfield names are correct across all build targets before removing fallback code.
Partial percentage matches in a PR body, such as 98.1% for ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC and ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8, are not enough evidence that an object should be promoted to Matching.
A configure.py matching-status change can be risky if the file still appears elsewhere as NonMatching; duplicate object entries should be checked carefully.
Generated or batched decomp PRs need source-level build validation before batching, because downstream slices may inherit broken commits.
naming_conventions: Popo fighter functions use the ftPp_ prefix, such as ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter and ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC.
Common fighter damage routines use the ftCo_ prefix, such as ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag.
Inishie stage routines use the grInishie1_ prefix for named functions and fn_ addresses for still-unknown local routines.
Named Fighter fields in this slice include x670_timer_lstick_tilt_x, x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y, x221F_b4, cmd_vars, mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0, fv.pp.x223C, and fv.pp.x2240.
The PR kept address-suffixed names for not-yet-semantically-named routines, such as ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0 and grInishie1_801FCAAC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use exact float literal and argument ordering in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls; the changed SpecialHi helpers use combinations of 0.0f, 1.0f, Ft_MF_None, and NULL.
For two adjacent accepted integer values, an unsigned subtraction range check like `(u32)(joint - 0x14) > 1` can reproduce compact compiler control flow.
Introducing intermediate float temporaries for squared components can change expression scheduling and may help match generated assembly.
Replacing raw M2C_FIELD macros with named fields can improve source quality but may perturb matching if the generated access pattern or types differ.
Dat attribute access was still expressed through a byte-offset cast, `*(float*)((u8*)da + 0x84)`, indicating the relevant ftIceClimberAttributes field was not yet named in this slice.
review_feedback: The only explicit maintainer/author feedback in the context was: "Closing - worktree commits have build issues that need to be fixed at the source before batching."
No line review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR was closed without merge, so these changes should be treated as an attempted matching batch rather than accepted project state.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that successfully matched ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter or ftPp_SpecialS.c after this closed batch.
Check whether ftIceClimberAttributes offset 0x84 was later given a semantic field name.
Search for configure.py duplicate entries for ftPp_SpecialS.c around PR 2020 to determine whether the Matching/NonMatching duplication caused or reflected the build issue.
Look for a later accepted implementation of grInishie1_801FCAAC and compare the unsigned joint range check.
Audit ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag history to confirm whether the >= stick threshold change was accepted or reverted.
